rows.finalText: Across "How do I commit to the path that feels deeply aligned", Purpose / Calling with a commitment emphasis gives the clearest frame. Clover House holds Counterpart (28), keeping the other person's position, their motives, and the part of the story you do not control directly in focus. On the ground, this is a vocation question where meaning, sustainability, and the right path are not identical things. rows.finalText: For "What is the next right step in my calling", the clearest pattern sits in Purpose / Calling with a right path emphasis. Anchor (35) in Past sets the tone with durability and follow-through that can actually hold. Anchor colors your calling through the version of vocation that can actually be sustained, not just admired from a distance. Present centers on Mice (23), where small drains can undermine major goals becomes the point that can redirect the whole sequence. Mice often points to the small repeated drain that weakens conviction or meaning if it is left unaddressed in your calling. Looking ahead, Future with House (4) points toward the need to focus on home base, habits, and emotional grounding, if the middle-card lesson is handled with care. House colors your calling through base structure, fixed conditions, and the foundations that have to hold before anything else can stretch. Anchor (35) with Mice (23) show what most shapes the question of calling: the risk of long-term depletion through unnoticed stress. The spread turns on Mice (23) more than any other card. Here, Mice emphasizes the small repeated drain that weakens conviction or meaning if it is left unaddressed. Anchor (35) with Mice (23) keep emphasizing the risk of long-term depletion through unnoticed stress. Patch leaks and simplify so House (4) can land as lived experience rather than just possibility.
